title: A New Way of Thinking
wikitext:
[]: A New Way of Thinking Post by CorporalChaos » Fri Feb 05, 2010 1:57 am To understand why things happen in the show that would not normally make sense, we have to consider the backstory of Evangelion. To do this requires a bit more cleverness in reading between the lines than has been previously demonstrated by the local fandom. Shinji is hated within the series because it turns out he's the product of a complicated love triangle involving Gendo, Yui, and Fuyutsuki. Yui has an affair with Fuyutsuki, and becomes pregnant by him. Gendo acts okay with this, but is really seething under the surface. When the contact experiment occurs, he slightly alters the parameters of the systems running the experiment, causing Yui to "die". After this turn of events, Gendo believes he has eliminated the treacherous hypotenuse, and remains Fuyutsuki's friend, as he believes that his old teacher is the path to greater power. However, he realizes that Shinji is not his son, and sends him away to inflict as much emotional trauma on his boy as Yui did to him. He also becomes aquainted with Naoko Akagi, and they shack up together. It's about this time that he comes to create Rei, and is essentially using her to replace Yui. However, Yui, still alive, somewhere in the systems, corrupts/possesses Rei, and baits Naoko into killing her. Gendo, enraged that Yui has been stolen from him a second time, arranges her "accidental" death. Yui also realizes that Rei is intended to become her replacement, so attempts to kill her as well by forcing Unit 00 to become berserk in a controlled setting. This is the main story that takes place in the years before the show, and what happens during the course of the series is the predictable play out of how these elements come together. The only actions that really need to contemplated during the course of the show are those of Fuyutsuki, who is secretly working with SEELE to deliver NERV into their hands, just because he's pissed at Gendo. It's getting late now, but I'll provide more evidence showing why this is true at some point. I just wanted to get the basic insight down while it was still fresh.

[]: Post by child of Lilith » Fri Feb 05, 2010 2:31 am Are you being serious? If you are, I’d really love to see your “evidence” to back up this claim, because to me this just sounds like more needless Gendo bashing.

[]: Post by Legendary » Fri Feb 05, 2010 2:45 am Evidence #1: Gendo is afraid of Shinji, and is ultimately judged to be running away. From what? However, when taking this new and revolutionary frame of view into account, one realizes that he's running away from Yui's true feelings by trying to bring her back. Evidence #2: The look Yui gives Fuyutsuki after their reunion in Episode 21 is immensely similar to the look that she gives Shinji before he un-tangs. It is clear that she cares deeply for both of them. Evidence #3: However, she cruelly attacks Gendo in his tangification process, showing her deep satisfaction with him. It's clearly revenge, even in obsolete 'fanon' views, but this theory adds more to it. As we all know, Evangelion throws the baby out with the bathwater when it comes to character treatment (re: Ritsuko's rape, according to some), so this clearly fits better. I fully look forward to the rest of this theorem.

[]: Post by child of Lilith » Fri Feb 05, 2010 2:54 am You make some interesting points, but I still think this idea is one hell of a reach.

[]: Post by ZapX » Fri Feb 05, 2010 3:01 am I like this. GJ, CC.

[]: Re: A New Way of Thinking Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 05, 2010 3:11 am CorporalChaos wrote: Yui has an affair with Fuyutsuki, and becomes pregnant by him.

[]: Post by Legendary » Fri Feb 05, 2010 3:29 am Gendo and Fuyutsuki don't meet in 2001. They meet in 2002 (2001 was the year of hell that no one is willing to talk about). Fuyutsuki's shock over the marriage is because he was with Yui in the IMMEDIATE post impact (late September-early October), but she never mentioned the marriage.

[]: Post by JoeD80 » Fri Feb 05, 2010 3:37 am Fuyutsuki knew that Gendo and Yui were dating before Second Impact, when he goes hiking with her.

[]: Post by Legendary » Fri Feb 05, 2010 4:12 am Fuyutsuki knew (from his 2000 perspective) that she'd been dating him a year ago. In this scenario, he wouldn't have been informed by Yui that things had continued. Indeed, she wouldn't have mentioned him at all. He then runs into Gendo and is surprised that he is still alive, and more surprised that he and Yui were apparently a couple the whole time, even though he impregnated Yui during the immediate aftermath of 2I.

[]: Post by Halicat » Fri Feb 05, 2010 7:43 am That's a pretty interesting concept. I'd like to see what evidence you can give for it, as right now I can't really think of anything myself, since we never really see Fuyutsuki interacting with Shinji and I always wondered what possessed Gendo to abandon Shinji the way he did. It always seemed a bit too much what he did, even considering how he is.

[]: Re: A New Way of Thinking Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 05, 2010 10:59 am CorporalChaos wrote: cleverness in reading between the lines

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Feb 05, 2010 12:31 pm None of it makes sense to me within context of the series. Sorry.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 05, 2010 1:01 pm Shinji is hated within the series because it turns out he's the product of a complicated love triangle involving Gendo, Yui, and Fuyutsuki. Yui has an affair with Fuyutsuki, and becomes pregnant by him. Gendo acts okay with this, but is really seething under the surface. When the contact experiment occurs, he slightly alters the parameters of the systems running the experiment, causing Yui to "die".

[]: Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Feb 05, 2010 2:16 pm If any of this is true, then there are several glaring problems. Firstly, if Gendou is not Shinji's father, then the drama between the two which speaks so much to Evangelion's themes of estrangement and isolation becomes meaningless. If there was no point to the Gendou Shinji relationship, if was truly as trite as what is being suggested, then Evangelion is an empty shell of a work. Secondly, if Gendou really did kill Yui, then his unrelenting desire to join with her in Unit-01 likewise makes no sense. Why would a man so enraged and betrayed by his wife go to such lengths to get her back? And if Gendou arranged for the contact experiment accident, then why would Yui have her conversation with Fuyutsuki about living in the Eva forever? Finally, if Fuyutsuki is working for SEELE, then he's doing a pretty poor job of it—remaining loyal to Gendou right up until the very end. While this thread is probably meant to be humorous, I sense it may becomes swiftly sidetracked into recent arguments about fan theories and speculation—especially concerning Ritsuko. I think we would all be a lot better off if people tried to keep discussions constructive rather than needlessly obstructive.

[]: Re: A New Way of Thinking Post by thewayneiac » Fri Feb 05, 2010 3:06 pm CorporalChaos wrote: Yui has an affair with Fuyutsuki, and becomes pregnant by him. ....... When the contact experiment occurs, he slightly alters the parameters of the systems running the experiment, causing Yui to "die".

[]: Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 05, 2010 3:19 pm Nice troll thread, CC...... All we need now are a few talking dinosaurs.

[]: Post by Uriel Septim VII » Fri Feb 05, 2010 5:49 pm Reichu wrote: Nice troll thread, CC...... All we need now are a few talking dinosaurs.

[]: Post by Lucretius » Fri Feb 05, 2010 9:39 pm Where does Pen Pen fit into all this? That's what I'd like to know. Also, I have a theory that Fuyutsuki is the one who raped Ritsuko.

[]: Post by ZapX » Fri Feb 05, 2010 10:09 pm I thought we made it clear that Earl Sinclair raped Ritsuko.

[]: Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 05, 2010 11:21 pm Well, if it's degenerated into a free-for-all already, I'd just like to mention that Kaworu is harboring a dysfunctional oviparous reproductive system and quite possibly a vagina. Also, Shinji and Asuka really had sex. Also, there are significant character parallels between Hyuga and Kensuke. I could let this thread run its course, but we've had enough trouble over the "Ritsuko / rape" thing recently, so nipping this in the bud early seems logical.
